title: psoralea prodiens
intro: Psoralea prodiens, also known as Kohu or New Zealand knawel, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea prodiens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has also been used in modern herbal preparations for its potential therapeutic benefits.
